  1390→
  1391→  const renderCard = (opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity, isDragging: boolean = false) => {
  1392→    const colors = STAGE_COLORS[opp.stage as SalesPipelineStage] || STAGE_COLORS.cold_dm;
  1393→    const bamfam = isBAMFAM(opp);
  1394→
  1395→    return (
  1396→      <Card
  1397→        className={`group hover:shadow-md transition-all duration-200 border-l-4 ${colors.border} ${isDragging ? 'shadow-lg ring-2 ring-blue-400 opacity-90' : ''} ${bamfam ? 'bg-red-50/30' : ''}`}
  1398→        onClick={() => openSlideOver(opp)}
  1399→      >
  1400→        <CardContent className="p-4">
  1401→          <div className="flex items-start justify-between gap-2">
  1402→            <div className="flex-1 min-w-0">
  1403→              {/* Name with drag handle */}
  1404→              <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
  1405→                <GripVertical className="h-4 w-4 text-gray-300 flex-shrink-0 cursor-grab active:cursor-grabbing" />
  1406→                <Building2 className="h-4 w-4 text-gray-400 flex-shrink-0" />
  1407→                <p className="font-medium text-gray-900 truncate">{opp.name}</p>
  1408→              </div>
  1409→
  1410→              {/* Deal Value */}
  1411→              {opp.deal_value && (
  1412→                <p className="text-lg font-semibold text-emerald-600 mt-2">
  1413→                  ${opp.deal_value.toLocaleString()}
  1414→                </p>
  1415→              )}
  1416→
  1417→              {/* Badges */}
  1418→              <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-1.5 mt-2">
  1419→                {opp.bucket && (
  1420→                  <Badge className={`text-xs ${BUCKET_COLORS[opp.bucket].bg} ${BUCKET_COLORS[opp.bucket].text} border-0`}>
  1421→                    Bucket {opp.bucket}
  1422→                  </Badge>
  1423→                )}
  1424→                {opp.dm_account === 'closer' && (
  1425→                  <Badge variant="outline" className="text-xs bg-white border-blue-300 text-blue-600">Closer</Badge>
  1426→                )}
  1427→                {opp.dm_account === 'sdr' && (
  1428→                  <Badge variant="outline" className="text-xs bg-white border-green-300 text-green-600">SDR</Badge>
  1429→                )}
  1430→                {opp.source && (
  1431→                  <Badge variant="outline" className="text-xs bg-white capitalize">
  1432→                    {opp.source.replace('_', ' ')}
  1433→                  </Badge>
  1434→                )}
  1435→              </div>
  1436→
  1437→              {/* Details */}
  1438→              <div className="mt-3 space-y-1.5 text-xs text-gray-500">
  1439→                {/* Bump progress (cold_dm only) */}
```

  3152→          </div>
  3153→        )}
  3154→
  3155→        {slideOverMode === 'view' && actionGuidance && (
  3156→          <div className="px-6 py-3 border-b border-sky-200" style={{ backgroundColor: '#f0f9fa' }}>
  3157→            <div className="flex items-start gap-2">
  3158→              <Zap className="h-4 w-4 flex-shrink-0 mt-0.5" style={{ color: '#3e8692' }} />
  3159→              <div>
  3160→                <p className="text-sm font-semibold" style={{ color: '#3e8692' }}>{actionGuidance.label}</p>
  3161→                <p className="text-xs text-gray-600 mt-0.5">{actionGuidance.hint}</p>
  3162→              </div>
  3163→              <Button
  3164→                variant="ghost"
  3165→                size="sm"
  3166→                className="h-6 w-6 p-0 ml-auto flex-shrink-0 text-gray-400 hover:text-gray-600"
  3167→                onClick={() => setActionGuidance(null)}
  3168→              >
  3169→                <X className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />
  3170→              </Button>
  3171→            </div>
  3172→          </div>
  3173→        )}
  3174→
  3175→        {slideOverMode === 'view' && (
  3176→        <ScrollArea className="flex-1">
  3177→          <div className="p-6 space-y-6">
  3178→            {/* Details Card */}
  3179→            <div className="bg-gray-50 rounded-lg border border-gray-200 p-4">
  3180→              <h4 className="text-xs font-semibold text-gray-500 uppercase tracking-wider mb-3">Details</h4>
  3181→              <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-x-4 gap-y-3 text-sm">
  3182→                <div>
  3183→                  <span className="text-xs text-gray-500">Temperature</span>
  3184→                  <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mt-1">
  3185→                    <div className="w-20 h-2 bg-gray-200 rounded-full overflow-hidden">
  3186→                      <div
  3187→                        className={`h-full rounded-full ${opp.temperature_score >= 70 ? 'bg-green-500' : opp.temperature_score >= 40 ? 'bg-amber-500' : 'bg-red-400'}`}
  3188→                        style={{ width: `${opp.temperature_score}%` }}
  3189→                      />
  3190→                    </div>
  3191→                    <span className="text-xs font-medium">{opp.temperature_score}%</span>
  3192→                  </div>
  3193→                </div>
  3194→                <div>
  3195→                  <span className="text-xs text-gray-500">Owner</span>
  3196→                  <p className="font-medium mt-0.5">{getUserName(opp.owner_id)}</p>
  3197→                </div>
  3198→                <div>
  3199→                  <span className="text-xs text-gray-500">POC</span>

```text
     1→import * as React from 'react';
     2→import { Slot } from '@radix-ui/react-slot';
     3→import { cva, type VariantProps } from 'class-variance-authority';
     4→
     5→import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
     6→
     7→const buttonVariants = cva(
     8→  'inline-flex items-center justify-center whitespace-nowrap rounded-md text-sm font-medium ring-offset-background transition-colors focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-[#3e8692] focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50',
     9→  {
    10→    variants: {
    11→      variant: {
    12→        default: 'bg-primary text-primary-foreground hover:bg-primary/90',
    13→        destructive:
    14→          'bg-destructive text-destructive-foreground hover:bg-destructive/90',
    15→        outline:
    16→          'border border-input bg-background hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground',
    17→        secondary:
    18→          'bg-secondary text-secondary-foreground hover:bg-secondary/80',
    19→        ghost: 'hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground',
    20→        link: 'text-primary underline-offset-4 hover:underline',
    21→      },
    22→      size: {
    23→        default: 'h-10 px-4 py-2',
    24→        sm: 'h-9 rounded-md px-3',
    25→        lg: 'h-11 rounded-md px-8',
    26→        icon: 'h-10 w-10',
    27→      },
    28→    },
    29→    defaultVariants: {
    30→      variant: 'default',
    31→      size: 'default',
    32→    },
    33→  }
    34→);
    35→
    36→export interface ButtonProps
    37→  extends React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>,
    38→    VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> {
    39→  asChild?: boolean;
    40→}
    41→
    42→const Button = React.forwardRef<HTMLButtonElement, ButtonProps>(
    43→  ({ className, variant, size, asChild = false, ...props }, ref) => {
    44→    const Comp = asChild ? Slot : 'button';
    45→    return (
    46→      <Comp
    47→        className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))}
    48→        ref={ref}
    49→        {...props}
    50→      />
    51→    );
    52→  }
    53→);
    54→Button.displayName = 'Button';
    55→
    56→export { Button, buttonVariants };
    57→
```
</details></tool-use>

    18→import { useToast } from '@/hooks/use-toast';
    19→import { FormService, FormWithFields, FormField, FieldType, FormStatus, FormResponse } from '@/lib/formService';
    20→import { CustomColorPicker } from '@/components/ui/custom-color-picker';
    21→import { DndContext, closestCenter, KeyboardSensor, PointerSensor, useSensor, useSensors, DragEndEvent } from '@dnd-kit/core';
    22→import { arrayMove, SortableContext, sortableKeyboardCoordinates, verticalListSortingStrategy, horizontalListSortingStrategy, useSortable } from '@dnd-kit/sortable';
    23→import { CSS } from '@dnd-kit/utilities';

    26→
    27→// Sortable Field Item Component
    28→interface SortableFieldItemProps {
    29→  field: FormField;
    30→  handleOpenFieldDialog: (field: FormField) => void;
    31→  handleDeleteField: (id: string, label: string) => void;
    32→  editingFieldId: string | null;
    33→  setEditingFieldId: (id: string | null) => void;
    34→  onSaveField: (fieldId: string, updates: Partial<FormField>) => Promise<void>;
    35→}
    36→
    37→function SortableFieldItem({ field, handleOpenFieldDialog, handleDeleteField, editingFieldId, setEditingFieldId, onSaveField }: SortableFieldItemProps) {
    38→  const {
    39→    attributes,
    40→    listeners,
    41→    setNodeRef,
    42→    transform,
    43→    transition,
    44→    isDragging,
    45→  } = useSortable({ id: field.id });
    46→
    47→  const style = {
    48→    transform: CSS.Transform.toString(transform),
    49→    transition,
    50→    opacity: isDragging ? 0.5 : 1,
    51→  };
    52→
    53→  const isEditing = editingFieldId === field.id;
    54→  const [editLabel, setEditLabel] = useState(field.label);
    55→  const [editRequired, setEditRequired] = useState(field.required);
    56→  const [editFieldType, setEditFieldType] = useState<FieldType>(field.field_type);
    57→  const [editAllowMultiple, setEditAllowMultiple] = useState(field.allow_multiple || false);
    58→  const [editIncludeOther, setEditIncludeOther] = useState(field.include_other || false);
    59→  const [editAllowAttachments, setEditAllowAttachments] = useState(field.allow_attachments || false);
    60→  const [editRequireYesReason, setEditRequireYesReason] = useState(field.require_yes_reason || false);
    61→  const [editRequireNoReason, setEditRequireNoReason] = useState(field.require_no_reason || false);
    62→  const [editOptions, setEditOptions] = useState<string[]>(field.options || []);
    63→  const [isYesNoDropdown, setIsYesNoDropdown] = useState(field.is_yes_no_dropdown || false);
    64→  const [newOption, setNewOption] = useState('');
    65→  const labelEditRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
    66→  const [showColorPicker, setShowColorPicker] = useState(false);
    67→  const [showCustomColorPicker, setShowCustomColorPicker] = useState(false);
    68→  const [currentColor, setCurrentColor] = useState('#000000');
    69→  const [savedSelection, setSavedSelection] = useState<Range | null>(null);
    70→
    71→  // Sync edit state when field changes
    72→  useEffect(() => {
    73→    setEditLabel(field.label);
    74→    setEditRequired(field.required);
    75→    setEditFieldType(field.field_type);
    76→    setEditAllowMultiple(field.allow_multiple || false);
    77→    setEditIncludeOther(field.include_other || false);
    78→    setEditAllowAttachments(field.allow_attachments || false);
    79→    setEditRequireYesReason(field.require_yes_reason || false);
    80→    setEditRequireNoReason(field.require_no_reason || false);
    81→    setEditOptions(field.options || []);
    82→    setIsYesNoDropdown(field.is_yes_no_dropdown || false);
    83→  }, [field.label, field.required, field.field_type, field.allow_multiple, field.include_other, field.allow_attachments, field.require_yes_reason, field.require_no_reason, field.is_yes_no_dropdown, field.options, field.id]);
    84→
    85→  // Focus label input when entering edit mode
    86→  useEffect(() => {
    87→    if (isEditing && labelEditRef.current) {
    88→      labelEditRef.current.focus();
    89→      labelEditRef.current.innerHTML = editLabel;
    90→    }
    91→  }, [isEditing]);
    92→
    93→  // Helper functions for text selection
    94→  const saveSelection = () => {
    95→    const selection = window.getSelection();
    96→    if (selection && selection.rangeCount > 0) {
    97→      setSavedSelection(selection.getRangeAt(0));
    98→    }
    99→  };
   100→
   101→  const restoreSelection = () => {
   102→    if (savedSelection) {
   103→      const selection = window.getSelection();
   104→      if (selection) {
   105→        selection.removeAllRanges();
   106→        selection.addRange(savedSelection);
   107→      }
   108→    }
   109→  };
   110→
   111→  const applyColorToSelection = (color: string) => {
   112→    if (!labelEditRef.current) return;
   113→    labelEditRef.current.focus();
   114→    restoreSelection();
   115→    const selection = window.getSelection();
   116→    if (selection && selection.rangeCount > 0 && !selection.isCollapsed) {
   117→      document.execCommand('foreColor', false, color);
   118→    } else {
   119→      document.execCommand('foreColor', false, color);
   120→    }
   121→  };
   122→
   123→  const changeFontSize = (increase: boolean) => {
   124→    if (!labelEditRef.current) return;
   125→
   126→    const selection = window.getSelection();
   127→    if (!selection || selection.rangeCount === 0) return;
   128→
   129→    const range = selection.getRangeAt(0);
   130→    if (range.collapsed) return; // No text selected
   131→
   132→    // Get the parent element of the selection
   133→    const parentElement = range.commonAncestorContainer.nodeType === Node.TEXT_NODE
   134→      ? range.commonAncestorContainer.parentElement
   135→      : range.commonAncestorContainer as HTMLElement;
   136→
   137→    // Find if we're inside a span with fontSize (walk up the DOM)
   138→    let existingSpan: HTMLElement | null = null;
   139→    let current: HTMLElement | null = parentElement;
   140→
   141→    while (current && current !== labelEditRef.current) {
   142→      if (current.tagName === 'SPAN' && current.style.fontSize) {
   143→        existingSpan = current;
   144→        break;
   145→      }
   146→      current = current.parentElement;
   147→    }
   148→
   149→    // Determine current font size
   150→    let currentSize = 16; // Default

 `./lib/userService.ts`

```text
     1→import { supabase } from './supabase'
     2→import { Database } from './database.types'
     3→
     4→type UserProfile = Database['public']['Tables']['users']['Row']
     5→type UserInsert = Database['public']['Tables']['users']['Insert']
     6→type UserUpdate = Database['public']['Tables']['users']['Update']
     7→
     8→export class UserService {
     9→  /**
    10→   * Create a new user profile (for OAuth users)
    11→   */
    12→  static async createUserProfile(userData: UserInsert): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
    13→    try {
    14→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    15→        .from('users')
    16→        .insert(userData)
    17→        .select()
    18→        .single()
    19→
    20→      if (error) {
    21→        console.error('Error creating user profile:', error)
    22→        return null
    23→      }
    24→
    25→      return data
    26→    } catch (error) {
    27→      console.error('Error creating user profile:', error)
    28→      return null
    29→    }
    30→  }
    31→
    32→  /**
    33→   * Get or create user profile (useful for OAuth flows)
    34→   */
    35→  static async getOrCreateUserProfile(
    36→    userId: string,
    37→    email: string,
    38→    name?: string
    39→  ): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
    40→    // First try to get existing profile
    41→    const existingProfile = await this.getUserProfile(userId)
    42→    if (existingProfile) {
    43→      return existingProfile
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    // Create new profile for OAuth user — inactive until admin approves
    47→    return this.createUserProfile({
    48→      id: userId,
    49→      email,
    50→      name: name || email.split('@')[0],
    51→      role: 'member',
    52→      is_active: false,
    53→    })
    54→  }
    55→
    56→  /**
    57→   * Get user profile by ID
    58→   */
    59→  static async getUserProfile(userId: string): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
    60→    try {
    61→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    62→        .from('users')
    63→        .select('*')
    64→        .eq('id', userId)
    65→        .single()
    66→
    67→      if (error) {
    68→        console.error('Error fetching user profile:', error)
    69→        return null
    70→      }
    71→
    72→      return data
    73→    } catch (error) {
    74→      console.error('Error fetching user profile:', error)
    75→      return null
    76→    }
    77→  }
    78→
    79→  /**
    80→   * Get all users (admin only)
    81→   */
    82→  static async getAllUsers(): Promise<UserProfile[]> {
    83→    try {
    84→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    85→        .from('users')
    86→        .select('*')
    87→        .order('created_at', { ascending: false })
    88→
    89→      if (error) {
    90→        console.error('Error fetching users:', error)
    91→        return []
    92→      }
    93→
    94→      return data || []
    95→    } catch (error) {
    96→      console.error('Error fetching users:', error)
    97→      return []
    98→    }
    99→  }
   100→
```
</details></tool-use>

  3920→              <Label className="text-xs font-semibold text-gray-500 uppercase tracking-wider">Title</Label>
  3921→              <Input
  3922→                value={activityLogForm.title}
  3923→                onChange={e => setActivityLogForm(f => ({ ...f, title: e.target.value }))}
  3924→                className="auth-input"
  3925→                placeholder="Activity title..."
  3926→              />
  3927→            </div>
  3928→
  3929→            {/* Meeting Date/Time pickers */}
  3930→            {activityLogPrompt?.showMeetingPicker && (
  3931→              <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4">
  3932→                <div className="grid gap-1.5">
  3933→                  <Label className="text-xs font-semibold text-gray-500 uppercase tracking-wider">Meeting Date</Label>
  3934→                  <Popover>
  3935→                    <PopoverTrigger asChild>
  3936→                      <Button
  3937→                        variant="outline"
  3938→                        className="auth-input justify-start text-left font-normal w-full"
  3939→                        style={{ borderColor: '#e5e7eb', backgroundColor: 'white', color: activityLogForm.meeting_date ? '#111827' : '#9ca3af' }}
  3940→                      >
  3941→                        <Calendar className="mr-2 h-4 w-4" />
  3942→                        {meetingDateObj ? format(meetingDateObj, 'MMM d, yyyy') : 'Select date'}
  3943→                      </Button>
  3944→                    </PopoverTrigger>
  3945→                    <PopoverContent className="!bg-white border shadow-md p-0 w-auto z-[90]" align="start">
  3946→                      <CalendarPicker
  3947→                        mode="single"
  3948→                        selected={meetingDateObj}
  3949→                        onSelect={date => {
  3950→                          setActivityLogForm(f => ({ ...f, meeting_date: date ? date.toISOString() : undefined }));
  3951→                        }}
  3952→                        initialFocus
  3953→                        classNames={{ day_selected: 'text-white hover:text-white focus:text-white' }}
  3954→                        modifiersStyles={{ selected: { backgroundColor: '#3e8692' } }}
  3955→                      />
  3956→                    </PopoverContent>
  3957→                  </Popover>
  3958→                </div>
  3959→                <div className="grid gap-1.5">
  3960→                  <Label className="text-xs font-semibold text-gray-500 uppercase tracking-wider">Meeting Time</Label>
  3961→                  <Popover>
  3962→                    <PopoverTrigger asChild>
  3963→                      <Button
  3964→                        variant="outline"

  3967→                      >
  3968→                        <Clock className="mr-2 h-4 w-4" />
  3969→                        {activityLogForm.meeting_time
  3970→                          ? (() => { const [h, m] = activityLogForm.meeting_time!.split(':').map(Number); return `${h === 0 ? 12 : h > 12 ? h - 12 : h}:${String(m).padStart(2, '0')} ${h >= 12 ? 'PM' : 'AM'}`; })()
  3971→                          : 'Select time'}
  3972→                      </Button>
  3973→                    </PopoverTrigger>
  3974→                    <PopoverContent className="!bg-white border shadow-md p-0 w-auto z-[90]" align="start">
  3975→                      <div className="flex gap-0 divide-x">
  3976→                        <ScrollArea className="h-[200px] w-[70px]">
  3977→                          <div className="p-1">
  3978→                            {Array.from({ length: 24 }, (_, h) => {
  3979→                              const label = `${h === 0 ? 12 : h > 12 ? h - 12 : h} ${h >= 12 ? 'PM' : 'AM'}`;
  3980→                              const isSelected = activityLogForm.meeting_time && parseInt(activityLogForm.meeting_time.split(':')[0]) === h;
  3981→                              return (
  3982→                                <Button
  3983→                                  key={h}
  3984→                                  variant="ghost"
  3985→                                  className={`w-full justify-center font-normal text-xs h-7 px-1 ${isSelected ? 'text-white hover:text-white' : ''}`}
  3986→                                  style={isSelected ? { backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' } : {}}
  3987→                                  onClick={() => {
  3988→                                    const currentMin = activityLogForm.meeting_time ? activityLogForm.meeting_time.split(':')[1] : '00';
  3989→                                    setActivityLogForm(f => ({ ...f, meeting_time: `${String(h).padStart(2, '0')}:${currentMin}` }));
  3990→                                  }}
  3991→                                >
  3992→                                  {label}
  3993→                                </Button>
  3994→                              );
  3995→                            })}
  3996→                          </div>
  3997→                        </ScrollArea>
  3998→                        <ScrollArea className="h-[200px] w-[50px]">
  3999→                          <div className="p-1">
  4000→                            {Array.from({ length: 60 }, (_, m) => {
  4001→                              const isSelected = activityLogForm.meeting_time && parseInt(activityLogForm.meeting_time.split(':')[1]) === m;

   277→    const closedLost = all.filter(o => o.stage === 'v2_closed_lost');
   278→    const inOrbit = all.filter(o => o.stage === 'orbit');
   279→
   280→    // Stages past cold_dm = responded
   281→    const pastColdDm = all.filter(o => o.stage !== 'cold_dm');
   282→    const coldDmCount = outreachTotal > 0 ? outreachTotal : all.filter(o => o.stage === 'cold_dm').length;
   283→    const totalDmsSent = all.length; // every opp started as a DM
   284→    const responseRate = totalDmsSent > 0 ? (pastColdDm.length / totalDmsSent) * 100 : 0;
   285→
   286→    // Meetings booked = in booked or any stage after it
   287→    const bookedAndBeyond = ['booked', 'discovery_done', 'proposal_call', 'v2_contract', 'v2_closed_won'];
   288→    const meetingsBooked = all.filter(o => bookedAndBeyond.includes(o.stage)).length;
   289→
   290→    // Discovery calls = in discovery_done or later
   291→    const discoveryAndBeyond = ['discovery_done', 'proposal_call', 'v2_contract', 'v2_closed_won'];
   292→    const discoveryCalls = all.filter(o => discoveryAndBeyond.includes(o.stage)).length;
   293→
   294→    // Close rate = won / (won + lost)

Let me read the schema file and search for the Calendly fields:

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>

`grep -A 200 "CREATE TABLE.*crm_opportunities" "/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/022_create_crm_tables.sql" | head -100`

```text
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_opportunities (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),

  -- Basic Info
  name TEXT NOT NULL,

  -- Pipeline Stage (unified lead + deal stages)
  stage TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'new',
  -- Lead stages: 'new', 'contacted', 'qualified', 'unqualified', 'nurture'
  -- Deal stages: 'proposal', 'contract', 'closed_won', 'closed_lost'

  -- Classification
  account_type TEXT, -- 'general', 'channel', 'campaign', 'lite', 'ad_hoc' (set when qualified)

  -- Value (set when becomes a deal)
  deal_value DECIMAL,
  currency TEXT DEFAULT 'USD',

  -- Tracking
  last_contacted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,

  -- Ownership & Source
  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  source TEXT, -- 'referral', 'inbound', 'event', 'cold_outreach'
  referrer TEXT, -- Who referred them

  -- Communication
  gc TEXT, -- Group Chat link/info

  -- Affiliate connection (for commission tracking)
  affiliate_id UUID REFERENCES crm_affiliates(id),

  -- Notes
  notes TEXT,

  -- Timestamps
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  qualified_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE, -- When moved from lead to deal stage
  closed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE -- When won or lost
);

-- ============================================
-- 3. PARTNERS TABLE
-- BD relationships, cross-partnerships
-- Lifecycle: Active / Inactive
-- ============================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_partners (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),

  -- Basic Info
  name TEXT NOT NULL,

  -- Classification
  category TEXT, -- 'service_provider', 'investor_vc', 'project', 'individual'
  focus TEXT, -- Area of focus/expertise

  -- Lifecycle
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', -- 'active', 'inactive'

  -- Tracking
  last_contacted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,

  -- Ownership
  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),

  -- Point of Contact (inline)
  poc_name TEXT,
  poc_email TEXT,
  poc_telegram TEXT,

  -- Affiliate connection (if partner is also an affiliate)
  is_affiliate BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  affiliate_id UUID REFERENCES crm_affiliates(id),

  -- Notes
  notes TEXT,

  -- Timestamps
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- ============================================
-- 4. CONTACTS TABLE
-- Individual people linked to opportunities/partners/affiliates
-- ============================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_contacts (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),

  -- Basic Info
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  email TEXT,
  telegram_id TEXT,
  x_id TEXT,
  role TEXT, -- Their role/title
  category TEXT, -- Contact category

  -- Ownership
  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-03-06 03:40:41Z)**_

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's a comprehensive analysis of how the sales pipeline handles meeting booking:

## Summary of Meeting Booking Workflow

### 1. **`next_meeting_at` Field Setup**

`next_meeting_at` is set in two primary ways:

**A. Via Activity Log Dialog (Main Flow)**
- Located: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx` (lines 3930-4024)
- When transitioning to `booked` or `proposal_call` stages, a meeting picker dialog appears
- User selects **date** and **time** separately via popovers:
  - Date picker uses calendar component (lines 3932-3957)
  - Time picker uses scrollable hour/minute columns (lines 3959-4023)
- The date and time are stored temporarily in `activityLogForm.meeting_date` and `activityLogForm.meeting_time`
- On confirmation, they're combined and saved as ISO string: `next_meeting_at: meetingDate.toISOString()` (line 1352)

**B. Via Edit Dialog**
- Located: lines 3019-3116
- Users can manually set meeting date/time when editing an opportunity
- Same date/time picker UI pattern

### 2. **Calendly Fields (`calendly_sent_via`, `calendly_sent_date`, `calendly_booked_date`)**

These fields are defined in the TypeScript type but are **NOT currently being set anywhere in the UI code**:

```typescript
// From lib/salesPipelineService.ts (lines 133-135)
calendly_sent_via: CalendlySentVia;           // 'dm' | 'tg' | 'not_yet'
calendly_sent_date: string | null;
calendly_booked_date: string | null;
```

**Current Usage:**
- Only reference found: Line 379 in page.tsx
  ```typescript
  if (o.calendly_booked_date && idx <= stageOrder['booked']) return true;
  ```
  This checks if a meeting was booked via Calendly link (used for ordering/filtering)
- **These fields are ready in the service layer but need booking UI implementation**

### 3. **`SalesPipelineOpportunity` Type - Meeting-Related Fields**

From `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/salesPipelineService.ts` (lines 125-147):

```typescript
export interface SalesPipelineOpportunity extends CRMOpportunity {
  // Meeting fields
  next_meeting_at: string | null;           // ISO datetime of next scheduled meeting
  next_meeting_type: string | null;         // 'discovery', 'proposal', 'follow_up', 'closing'
  
  // Calendly tracking (not yet UI-implemented)
  calendly_sent_via: CalendlySentVia;       // How Calendly was sent: 'dm' | 'tg' | 'not_yet'
  calendly_sent_date: string | null;        // When Calendly link was sent
  calendly_booked_date: string | null;      // When meeting was actually booked via Calendly
  
  // Other meeting-related timestamps
  discovery_call_at: string | null;         // Auto-set when stage → 'discovery_done'
  
  // Engagement tracking
  last_bump_date: string | null;
  last_contacted_at: string | null;
}
```

### 4. **Stage Transition to `booked`**

**How the `booked` stage transition works:**

1. **Trigger:** User clicks "Booked!" action (line 1063, 1298)
2. **Activity Log Prompt Opens** with `showMeetingPicker: true` (line 1305)
   ```typescript
   'booked': { type: 'meeting', title: 'Meeting booked', showMeetingPicker: true }
   ```
3. **Data Flow:**
   - User selects meeting date/time in dialog
   - On confirm: `handleStageChange()` (line 1292) → `SalesPipelineService.update()`
   - Meeting date/time combined and saved: `next_meeting_at = meetingDate.toISOString()` (line 1352)
   - Activity record created with type `'meeting'` (line 1334)

4. **Stage Validation:** 
   - Post-`booked` stages must have `next_meeting_at` set
   - BAMFAM violations trigger if no future meeting is scheduled (line 448-464)

### 5. **Database Schema - `crm_opportunities` Table**

The base schema (from `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/022_create_crm_tables.sql`) shows the table created without the meeting fields.

**Extended fields added via migration:**
- Migration `20260223124404` named "add_v2_columns_to_crm_opportunities" — adds:
  - `bucket`, `temperature_score`, `dm_account`, `bump_number`, `last_bump_date`
  - `warm_sub_state`, `tg_handle`, `gc_opened`
  - `orbit_reason`, `closed_lost_reason`, `dedup_key`, `next_meeting_at`, `next_meeting_type`
  - `bucket_changed_at`, `discovery_call_at`, `proposal_sent_at`
  - `poc_platform`, `poc_handle`

- Migration `20260226123457` named "add_poc_fields_to_opportunities" — adds POC-specific columns

**Note:** The Calendly fields (`calendly_sent_via`, `calendly_sent_date`, `calendly_booked_date`) are defined in the TypeScript type but appear to **not yet exist in the database schema**. They need a migration to be added.

---

## Key Insights for Your Booking Page

1. **Current Flow:** Meeting booking is tied to stage transitions — when you move to `booked` stage, you're prompted to set the meeting datetime
2. **Missing Piece:** Calendly integration fields exist in code but not in DB or UI
3. **For Your Booking Page, You'll Need:**
   - Add database migration to create `calendly_sent_via`, `calendly_sent_date`, `calendly_booked_date` columns
   - Create UI to capture when Calendly link is sent (via DM/TG) and when meeting is booked
   - Update `confirmActivityLog()` (line 1329) to auto-populate these fields when booking is confirmed
   - Hook into Calendly webhook to auto-update `calendly_booked_date` when attendee completes booking

4. **Auto-Update Pattern:** The codebase already has this pattern (line 300-314) where stage transitions auto-set timestamps — you can follow this for Calendly fields

Perfect! I now have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary report of my findings:

## Codebase Public Page Patterns & Architecture

Based on my thorough exploration, here's a comprehensive breakdown of the KOL Campaign Manager codebase:

### 1. PUBLIC FORMS PAGE ARCHITECTURE
**File Path:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/public/forms/[id]/page.tsx`

**Key Components:**
- **Data Fetching Pattern:** Uses Supabase public client (`supabasePublic = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey)`)
- **Form Loading:** `FormService.getPublicForm(formId, supabasePublic)` - fetches form by ID or slug
- **Layout & Structure:**
  - Multi-page support with page navigation (Previous/Next buttons)
  - Page indicator dots showing progress
  - Dynamic field rendering based on `field_type` and `page_number`
  
**Form Submission Flow:**
1. Validates current page (required fields, email format)
2. Handles file uploads to Supabase Storage (`form-attachments` bucket)
3. Submits via `/api/forms/submit` endpoint (uses service role to bypass RLS)
4. Supports conditional thank-you pages (when `enable_thank_you_page: true`)

**Field Types Supported:**
- `text`, `email`, `number`, `textarea` (with optional attachments)
- `select`, `radio`, `checkbox` (with "Other" option)
- `date` (with calendar picker)
- `section`, `description` (non-input fields)
- Optional features: `allow_multiple`, `include_other`, `allow_attachments`, `require_yes_reason`, `require_no_reason`

**Form Type Structure:**
```typescript
FormWithFields {
  id, user_id, name, slug, description, status, 
  subdomain_enabled, subdomain_url, created_at, updated_at,
  fields: FormField[]  // sorted by page_number then display_order
}
```

### 2. PUBLIC PORTAL PAGE ARCHITECTURE
**File Path:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx`

**Key Features:**
- Client authentication via email (cached for 24 hours locally)
- Displays client campaign information without requiring login
- Tabs for: All Campaigns, Active, Completed
- Shows meeting notes, client context, decision logs, weekly updates
- Public access to campaigns with `share_report_publicly: true`

**Data Types:**
```typescript
Client { id, name, email, slug, logo_url }
Campaign { id, name, slug, status, total_budget, start_date, end_date, region, kol_count, content_count, total_impressions, total_engagement, share_report_publicly }
MeetingNote { id, title, content, attendees, action_items, meeting_date, created_at }
ClientContext { engagement_type, scope, start_date, milestones, client_contacts, holohive_contacts }
WeeklyUpdate { id, week_of, current_focus, active_initiatives, next_checkin, open_questions }
```

**Layout:** Uses Supabase public client for unauthenticated access

### 3. EXISTING USERS IN DATABASE
Based on code references to team members in `/app/delivery-logs/page.tsx` and `/app/clients/[id]/delivery-log/page.tsx`:

**User Pattern:** Users are referenced by their `name` field in delivery logs and team selections. Code shows:
- `teamMembers` array of `{ id: string; name: string }`
- Toggle between "Pick from team" vs "Enter manually"
- Users stored in the `users` table with fields: `id, name, email, role, is_active, telegram_id, x_id, profile_photo_url, created_at, updated_at`

**Note:** The specific users "team", "yano", and "jdot" appear to be actual user names in the database, but I couldn't access the live DB. They're likely:
- Real team members referenced in tasks, delivery logs, and standup pages
- Could check `/app/tasks/page.tsx`, `/app/daily-standup/page.tsx`, or `/app/team/page.tsx` to see full user list

### 4. STYLING PATTERNS & BRAND COLORS

**Primary Brand Color:** `#3e8692` (teal/dark cyan)

**CSS Usage:**
- Inline styles: `style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}`
- Tailwind classes: `.text-[#3e8692]`, `.bg-[#3e8692]`, `.hover:text-[#3e8692]`
- Focus states: `focus:ring-[#3e8692]`, `focus:border-[#3e8692]`
- Calendar selected: `modifiersStyles={{ selected: { backgroundColor: "#3e8692" } }}`

**Global Focus Styles** (in `/app/globals.css`):
```css
.auth-input:focus {
  outline: none !important;
  border-color: #3e8692 !important;
  box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #3e8692 !important;
}
```

**Component Library:** shadcn/ui (via Radix UI primitives)
- Button: Variants (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link)
- Input: Standard HTML input with focus styling
- Card, Dialog, Tabs, Popover, Calendar, Badge, Select, etc.
- Uses `cn()` utility for className merging (from `@/lib/utils`)

### 5. SETTINGS PAGE
**File Path:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/settings/page.tsx`

**Features:**
- Profile information management (name, email, photo upload)
- Social media fields: `telegram_id`, `x_id`
- Telegram webhook integration for admins (manage, register, delete)
- Account information display (role, status, member since)
- Protected route (requires authentication)

**Data Stored:**
```typescript
{
  name: string,
  email: string (read-only),
  telegram_id?: string,
  x_id?: string,
  profile_photo_url?: string,
  role: 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'guest',
  is_active: boolean,
  created_at: string,
  updated_at: string
}
```

### 6. SUPABASE & DATABASE SETUP

**Connection:** 
- Browser client: `createBrowserClient<Database>(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey)` from `@supabase/ssr`
- Public client: `createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey)` from `@supabase/supabase-js`
- Server-side: Service role key for bypassing RLS

**Key Tables:**
- `forms` - contains form definitions with `enable_thank_you_page` column
- `form_fields` - fields with `page_number` and `display_order` for multi-page support
- `form_responses` - submitted responses
- `users` - team members
- `clients` - client information with slug support
- `campaigns` - campaign data with public share flag
- `client_notes`, `client_context`, `delivery_logs`, `tasks`, etc.

**File Uploads:** Stored in Supabase buckets:
- `form-attachments` - form submission files
- `profile-photos` - user profile pictures
- `campaign-report-files` - report attachments

### 7. FORM SERVICE METHODS
**File Path:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/formService.ts`

Key methods:
- `getPublicForm(idOrSlug, publicSupabase)` - for public access (no auth)
- `submitResponse(responseData, publicSupabase)` - submit via API route
- `getResponses(formId)` - fetch all responses for a form
- `exportResponsesToCSV(formId)` - export functionality
- Full CRUD for forms and fields with drag-and-drop reordering

---

**For your booking page feature**, you can follow:
1. **Layout Pattern:** Use `/app/public/forms/[id]/page.tsx` as template (public, unauthenticated, multi-step)
2. **Authentication:** Similar to `/app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx` (optional email verification)
3. **Styling:** Use `#3e8692` with teal/blue accents, shadcn components
4. **Data Structure:** Create booking-specific tables with similar patterns to forms/responses
5. **User References:** Look up team members from the `users` table by name or ID
6. **Settings:** Add booking preferences to the existing settings page

1. Primary Request and Intent:

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Supabase RLS policies, database triggers (`handle_new_user`, `notify_new_user_signup`), `pg_net` extension for HTTP calls
   - Supabase default 1000-row limit on queries — must paginate with `.range()` for large datasets
   - `crm_opportunities` table has two sets of stages: old CRM stages (`new`, `contacted`, `dead`) and v2 sales pipeline stages (`cold_dm`, `warm`, `tg_intro`, `booked`, `orbit`, etc.)
   - `SalesPipelineService.getAll()` filters by `ALL_V2_STAGES`; `getColdDmsPaginated()` uses `count: 'exact'` for accurate totals
   - `payments.content_id` is a `uuid[]` (array), not a single UUID
   - `dedup_key` has a conditional unique index (`WHERE dedup_key IS NOT NULL`) — `ON CONFLICT` doesn't work with partial unique indexes
   - Public page patterns: `/public/forms/[id]`, `/public/portal/[id]` — use `supabasePublic` client without auth
   - Existing meeting workflow: `next_meeting_at`, `next_meeting_type`, `calendly_sent_via`, `calendly_booked_date` fields on opportunities
   - Brand color: `#3e8692` (teal), shadcn/ui component library

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`lib/userService.ts`**
     - Removed `role` parameter from `getOrCreateUserProfile()`, hardcoded `role: 'member'` and `is_active: false`
     - Added `deleteUser()` method for rejecting pending sign-ups
     ```typescript
     static async getOrCreateUserProfile(
       userId: string,
       email: string,
       name?: string
     ): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
       const existingProfile = await this.getUserProfile(userId)
       if (existingProfile) return existingProfile
       return this.createUserProfile({
         id: userId, email,
         name: name || email.split('@')[0],
         role: 'member',
         is_active: false,
       })
     }

     static async deleteUser(userId: string): Promise<boolean> {
       try {
         const { error } = await supabase.from('users').delete().eq('id', userId)
         if (error) { console.error('Error deleting user:', error); return false }
         return true
       } catch (error) { console.error('Error deleting user:', error); return false }
     }
     ```

   - **`contexts/AuthContext.tsx`**
     - Removed the `'member'` role argument from `getOrCreateUserProfile()` call
     ```typescript
     const profile = await UserService.getOrCreateUserProfile(
       authUser.id,
       authUser.email || '',
       name
     )
     ```

   - **`components/auth/SignUpForm.tsx`**
     - Removed `Select` import, `role` state, role from sign-up metadata, role dropdown UI
     - Updated success message to mention admin approval
     ```typescript
     <CardDescription className="text-center">
       We've sent you a confirmation link. After verifying your email, an admin will review and approve your account.
     </CardDescription>
     ```

   - **`components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx`**
     - Complete rewrite: added `is_active` check after auth, renders "Pending Approval" screen with logo, Clock icon, teal branding, sign-out button for inactive users
     - Key section:
     ```tsx
     if (userProfile && userProfile.is_active === false) {
       return (
         <div className="min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center bg-gray-50">
           <div className="max-w-md w-full mx-4 text-center">
             {/* Logo, Clock icon, "Pending Approval" heading, message, Sign Out button */}
           </div>
         </div>
       )
     }
     ```

   - **`app/team/page.tsx`**
     - Complete rewrite: split users into `pendingMembers` and `activeMembers`, added amber-styled pending approval section with approve/reject buttons, added deactivate/remove buttons for active members
     - Key additions: `isAdmin` state (both admin + super_admin), `handleApprove`, `handleReject`, `handleDeactivate`, `handleDeleteMember` functions, `confirmDeleteId` confirmation state, `pendingRoles` for role assignment on approval

   - **`app/campaigns/page.tsx`**
     - Added `paidContentCounts` state and fetch logic
     - Fetch paid content counts in parallel with content counts, filtering against existing content IDs to avoid orphaned payment references
     - Added display line: `${paid} of ${counts.total} content paid`

   - **`lib/salesPipelineService.ts`**
     - Fixed `getAll()` to paginate past 1000-row Supabase limit:
     ```typescript
     static async getAll(): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity[]> {
       const pageSize = 1000;
       let allData: SalesPipelineOpportunity[] = [];
       let from = 0;
       let hasMore = true;
       while (hasMore) {
         const { data, error } = await supabase
           .from('crm_opportunities')
           .select(`*, affiliate:crm_affiliates(*), client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)`)
           .in('stage', ALL_V2_STAGES)
           .order('position', { ascending: true })
           .order('created_at', { ascending: false })
           .range(from, from + pageSize - 1);
         if (error) throw error;
         allData = allData.concat((data || []) as SalesPipelineOpportunity[]);
         hasMore = (data?.length || 0) === pageSize;
         from += pageSize;
       }
       return allData;
     }
     ```

   - **`app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`**
     - Added Pipeline tab badge count (excluding cold_dm)
     - Fixed Pipeline section count in Overall tab to exclude cold_dm
     - Fixed Outreach table in Overall tab to trigger `fetchOutreach` when section is expanded:
     ```typescript
     useEffect(() => {
       if (activeTab === 'outreach' || (activeTab === 'overview' && overviewSections.outreach)) {
         fetchOutreach();
       }
     }, [activeTab, fetchOutreach, overviewSections.outreach]);
     ```
     - Pipeline tab badge:
     ```tsx
     {(() => {
       const pipelineCount = opportunities.filter(o =>
         PIPELINE_STAGES.includes(o.stage as SalesPipelineStage) && o.stage !== 'cold_dm'
       ).length;
       return pipelineCount > 0 ? <Badge variant="secondary" className="ml-1">{pipelineCount}</Badge> : null;
     })()}
     ```

   - **Database Migrations Applied** (via Supabase MCP):
     1. `signup_approval_flow`: Changed `is_active` default to false, updated `handle_new_user()` trigger to hardcode `role='member'` and `is_active=false`, added `notify_new_user_signup()` trigger via `net.http_post` to Telegram (bot token, chat_id `-1002681460515`, thread_id 2)
     2. `add_admin_delete_users_policy`: Added `"Admins can delete users"` RLS policy using `is_admin_or_super_admin()`

   - **CSV Import** (via Supabase MCP execute_sql):
     - 1574 unique opportunities imported from `docs/Philton BD Tracker.csv`
     - Stage mapping: Cold→cold_dm, Warm-Interested→warm (sub_state: interested), Warm-Silent→warm (sub_state: silent), TG→tg_intro, Orbit→orbit, Closed→v2_closed_lost
     - Fields mapped: name, stage, poc_platform (twitter), poc_handle (extracted from X URL), last_contacted_at, bump_number, tg_handle, orbit_reason, warm_sub_state, notes, bucket (A/B from Opener), dm_account (sdr), dedup_key (philton_project_handle)

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **Content paid count > total content**: Payments referenced content IDs that no longer exist in the contents table. Fixed by intersecting paid content IDs with actual campaign content IDs using a `contentIds` Set.
   - **CSV duplicate dedup_keys**: 53 duplicate keys within the CSV itself (same POC appearing twice for same project). Fixed by deduplicating before import (1628→1574).
   - **`ON CONFLICT (dedup_key) DO NOTHING` failed**: The unique index on dedup_key is conditional (`WHERE dedup_key IS NOT NULL`), so ON CONFLICT doesn't work. Fixed by deduplicating in Python and using plain INSERT without ON CONFLICT.
   - **Overall tab count lower than Outreach tab**: `getAll()` hit Supabase's default 1000-row limit, returning only 1000 of 1574 opportunities. Outreach count used `count: 'exact'` which returned true count. Fixed by adding pagination loop in `getAll()`.
   - **Pipeline tab count included cold_dm**: `activeDeals` metric included cold_dm stage, but Pipeline tab's `visiblePipelineStages` excludes cold_dm. Fixed both the tab badge and the Overall tab's Pipeline section count to filter out cold_dm.
   - **Outreach table empty in Overall tab**: `fetchOutreach` only triggered when `activeTab === 'outreach'`, not when expanding the Outreach section in the Overview tab. Fixed by adding `overviewSections.outreach` to the useEffect condition.
   - **User corrected wrong CRM table**: Initially looked at old CRM pipeline stages (`new`, `contacted`, `dead`) instead of v2 sales pipeline stages (`cold_dm`, `warm`, `tg_intro`, etc.). User pointed out sales-pipeline page was empty and the old CRM was different. Corrected to use v2 stages.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Supabase RLS policies, database triggers (`handle_new_user`, `notify_new_user_signup`), `pg_net` extension for HTTP calls
   - Supabase default 1000-row limit on queries — must paginate with `.range()` for large datasets
   - `crm_opportunities` table has two sets of stages: old CRM stages (`new`, `contacted`, `dead`) and v2 sales pipeline stages (`cold_dm`, `warm`, `tg_intro`, `booked`, `orbit`, etc.)
   - `SalesPipelineService.getAll()` filters by `ALL_V2_STAGES`; `getColdDmsPaginated()` uses `count: 'exact'` for accurate totals
   - `payments.content_id` is a `uuid[]` (array), not a single UUID
   - `dedup_key` has a conditional unique index (`WHERE dedup_key IS NOT NULL`) — `ON CONFLICT` doesn't work with partial unique indexes
   - Public page patterns: `/public/forms/[id]`, `/public/portal/[id]` — use `supabasePublic` client without auth
   - Existing meeting workflow: `next_meeting_at`, `next_meeting_type`, `calendly_sent_via`, `calendly_booked_date` fields on opportunities
   - Brand color: `#3e8692` (teal), shadcn/ui component library

     ```typescript
     static async getOrCreateUserProfile(
       userId: string,
       email: string,
       name?: string
     ): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
       const existingProfile = await this.getUserProfile(userId)
       if (existingProfile) return existingProfile
       return this.createUserProfile({
         id: userId, email,
         name: name || email.split('@')[0],
         role: 'member',
         is_active: false,
       })
     }

     static async deleteUser(userId: string): Promise<boolean> {
       try {
         const { error } = await supabase.from('users').delete().eq('id', userId)
         if (error) { console.error('Error deleting user:', error); return false }
         return true
       } catch (error) { console.error('Error deleting user:', error); return false }
     }
     ```

```text
     1→# User Sign-Up Approval Flow
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→Anyone can currently sign up and immediately access all app data. The sign-up form even lets users self-assign roles (Admin/Member/Client). This needs to be locked down so admins control who gets access.
     5→
     6→## Approach
     7→Use the existing `is_active` field on the `users` table. New sign-ups default to `is_active = false`. ProtectedRoute blocks inactive users with a "Pending Approval" screen. Admins approve/reject from the Team page. Telegram notification fires on new sign-ups.
     8→
     9→## Changes
    10→
    11→### 1. Database Migration (Supabase MCP)
    12→- Change `is_active` column default from `true` to `false`
    13→- Update `handle_new_user()` trigger: hardcode `role = 'member'`, `is_active = false` (closes role self-assignment hole)
    14→- Add `notify_new_user_signup()` trigger via `net.http_post` to Telegram (same pattern as `notify_contact_submission`)
    15→- Existing users unaffected (already `is_active = true`)
    16→
    17→### 2. `lib/userService.ts`
    18→- `getOrCreateUserProfile()`: set `is_active: false`, hardcode `role: 'member'`
    19→
    20→### 3. `contexts/AuthContext.tsx`
    21→- Remove role argument from `getOrCreateUserProfile()` call
    22→
    23→### 4. `components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx`
    24→- Add `is_active` check after auth check
    25→- If `is_active === false`, render "Pending Approval" screen with sign-out button
    26→- Teal branding, logo, clear messaging
    27→
    28→### 5. `components/auth/SignUpForm.tsx`
    29→- Remove role `<Select>` dropdown
    30→- Remove role from sign-up metadata
    31→- Update success message to mention admin approval
    32→
    33→### 6. `app/team/page.tsx`
    34→- Split users into pending (`is_active = false`) and active lists
    35→- Pending section at top with amber styling
    36→- Each pending card: name, email, sign-up date, role selector, Approve (teal) + Reject (red) buttons
    37→- Approve → `UserService.activateUser()`, Reject → delete user row
    38→- Both admin and super_admin can approve
    39→
    40→## Verification
    41→1. Sign up with new email → email confirmation → "Pending Approval" screen after login
    42→2. Telegram notification fires with name/email + link to Team page
    43→3. Team page shows pending user with Approve/Reject buttons
    44→4. Approve → user can access the app
    45→5. Existing users → no change
    46→
```

```text
I now have a comprehensive picture of all the patterns. Let me compile the complete findings.

---

Here is a thorough breakdown of all five areas you asked about.

---

## 1. How Public Pages Work

**File tree under `/app/public/`:**
```
app/public/
  layout.tsx                    # Root public layout (no auth, robots noindex)
  page.tsx                      # Index page
  forms/[id]/
    layout.tsx                  # Fetches form name for <head> metadata
    page.tsx                    # Full form renderer (client component)
  portal/[id]/
    layout.tsx                  # Minimal layout
    page.tsx                    # Client portal viewer (client component)
  campaigns/[id]/               # Public campaign view
  reports/[id]/                 # Public report view
  lists/[id]/                   # Public list view
  leads/submit/page.tsx         # Lead submission form (no auth required)
  links/submit/page.tsx         # Link submission form (no auth required)
  partners/submit/page.tsx      # Partner submission form (no auth required)
  standup/submit/page.tsx       # Stand-up form (REQUIRES auth - redirects to /auth)
```

**Auth handling — the two patterns:**

**Pattern A: Fully public (no auth needed).** Used by `forms/[id]`, `leads/submit`, `links/submit`, `partners/submit`, `portal/[id]`:
- A raw `createClient(url, anonKey)` is created inline at the top of the client component — this is the "supabasePublic" client.
- No session check is done. The anon key's RLS policies gate what data can be read/written.
- Named `supabasePublic` in form and portal pages.

```ts
// Pattern A — from app/public/forms/[id]/page.tsx and portal/[id]/page.tsx
const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!;
const supabaseAnonKey = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!;
const supabasePublic = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey);
// import is: import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
```

The layout files use the same bare `createClient` for SSR metadata generation (e.g., fetching the form name in `generateMetadata`).

**Pattern B: Auth-gated public route.** Used by `standup/submit`:
- Uses the shared `supabase` browser client from `@/lib/supabase` (cookie-based, session-aware).
- Imports `useAuth` from `@/contexts/AuthContext`.
- Redirects unauthenticated users: `router.push('/auth?redirectTo=/public/standup/submit')`.

**Form submission approaches:**
- `forms/[id]` calls `FormService.submitResponse(...)` passing the `supabasePublic` client directly.
- `leads/submit` POSTs to `/api/leads/submit` (an internal Next.js API route) — the API route handles the actual Supabase insert with server-side privileges.
- `links/submit` and `partners/submit` use the inline `createClient` directly.

**Root public layout** (`/app/public/layout.tsx`): Minimal — just `min-h-screen bg-gray-50`, no nav, no auth context. Sets `robots: { index: false }` to block search engines.

---

## 2. Users Table Structure

**From `/lib/database.types.ts` (line 914):**

```ts
users: {
  Row: {
    id: string                    // UUID (matches Supabase auth.users.id)
    email: string
    name: string
    role: string                  // 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'guest'
    is_active: boolean
    profile_photo_url: string | null
    telegram_id: string | null
    x_id: string | null
    created_at: string | null
    updated_at: string | null
  }
  // Insert/Update types mirror the Row with optionals
}
```

**Notable: there is NO `slug` column on `users`.** The `slug` concept exists on `clients` (from the portal page's `Client` type: `slug: string | null`), not users.

**How users are fetched and listed:**

In `UserService` (`/lib/userService.ts`):
- `getAllUsers()` — fetches all rows from `users`, ordered by `created_at desc`
- `getUserProfile(userId)` — fetches one user by UUID
- `getCurrentUserProfile()` — calls `supabase.auth.getUser()` then `getUserProfile`
- Role hierarchy: `guest < member < admin < super_admin`
- New OAuth sign-ups are created with `is_active: false`, require admin approval

In the sales pipeline, users are used for owner dropdowns:
```ts
// From sales-pipeline/page.tsx
const [users, setUsers] = useState<{ id: string; name: string | null; email: string }[]>([]);
// ...
UserService.getAllUsers()  // returns full UserProfile[], used for owner assignment UI
```

The `TeamMember` interface in `app/team/page.tsx` has: `id, name, email, role, is_active, telegram_id, x_id, profile_photo_url`.

---

## 3. Sales Pipeline Opportunity Meeting Fields

**From `/lib/salesPipelineService.ts`:**

The `SalesPipelineOpportunity` interface **extends** `CRMOpportunity` with these meeting/calendly fields:

```ts
export interface SalesPipelineOpportunity extends CRMOpportunity {
  // Calendly tracking fields
  calendly_sent_via: CalendlySentVia;        // 'dm' | 'tg' | 'not_yet'
  calendly_sent_date: string | null;          // ISO datetime
  calendly_booked_date: string | null;        // ISO datetime — when they confirmed a slot

  // Next meeting scheduling
  next_meeting_at: string | null;             // ISO datetime — when next meeting is
  next_meeting_type: string | null;           // free-form string (e.g., 'discovery', 'proposal')

  // Other pipeline-specific timestamps
  bucket_changed_at: string | null;
  discovery_call_at: string | null;           // Auto-set when stage -> discovery_done
  proposal_sent_at: string | null;            // Auto-set when stage -> proposal_sent

  // Other fields
  bucket: Bucket | null;                      // 'A' | 'B' | 'C'
  temperature_score: number;
  dm_account: DmAccount;                      // 'closer' | 'sdr' | 'other'
  bump_number: number;
  last_bump_date: string | null;
  warm_sub_state: WarmSubState | null;        // 'interested' | 'silent'
  tg_handle: string | null;
  gc_opened: string;
  orbit_reason: OrbitReason | null;
  closed_lost_reason: string | null;
  dedup_key: string | null;
  poc_platform: PocPlatform | null;
  poc_handle: string | null;
}
```

**The `update()` method signature** includes all meeting fields:
```ts
static async update(id, updates: Partial<...> & {
  next_meeting_at?: string | null;
  next_meeting_type?: string | null;
  calendly_sent_via?: CalendlySentVia;
  calendly_sent_date?: string | null;
  calendly_booked_date?: string | null;
  // ...
})
```

**BAMFAM enforcement:** The service has `getBAMFAMViolations()` — it queries `discovery_done`, `proposal_call`, `v2_contract` stage opportunities where `next_meeting_at IS NULL` or `next_meeting_at < now()`. The metrics also count this number for the dashboard.

**`CalendlySentVia` type:** `'dm' | 'tg' | 'not_yet'` — tracks whether the Calendly link was sent via DM, Telegram, or hasn't been sent.

The underlying DB table is `crm_opportunities` (shared with the general CRM, filtered to sales pipeline v2 stages).

---

## 4. Settings Pages / Admin Config Patterns

**Only one settings page found:** `/app/settings/page.tsx`

Pattern used:
```ts
// Wrapped in ProtectedRoute — only accessible to authenticated users
export default function SettingsPage() {
  return (
    <ProtectedRoute>
      <SettingsContent />
    </ProtectedRoute>
  );
}

// Inner component uses the session-aware browser client
function SettingsContent() {
  const { user, userProfile, refreshUserProfile } = useAuth();
  const { toast } = useToast();

  // Profile fields editable: name, email, telegram_id, x_id, profile_photo_url
  // Admin/super_admin also see a Telegram webhook management section
}
```

The settings page uses `supabase` from `@/lib/supabase` (the cookie-based browser client), not `supabasePublic`. It calls Supabase auth methods (`supabase.auth.getSession()`) and passes the access token to internal API routes for privileged operations like webhook management.

---

## 5. Supabase Client Setup in `/lib/`

Three client variants exist:

**`/lib/supabase.ts` — Browser/client-side (auth-aware):**
```ts
import { createBrowserClient } from '@supabase/ssr'
import { Database } from './database.types'

// Uses @supabase/ssr — cookie-based session, works across client/server boundary
export const supabase = createBrowserClient<Database>(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey)
```
Used in: all `'use client'` components that need the user's session (settings, standup, CRM pages, team page).

**`/lib/supabase-server.ts` — Server-side (API routes, Server Components):**
```ts
import { createServerClient as createSupabaseServerClient } from '@supabase/ssr';
import { cookies } from 'next/headers';

export async function createServerClient() {
  const cookieStore = await cookies();
  return createSupabaseServerClient<Database>(url, anonKey, {
    cookies: {
      getAll() { return cookieStore.getAll(); },
      setAll(cookiesToSet) { /* sets cookies, ignores errors in Server Components */ }
    }
  });
}
```
Used in: API routes (`/api/...`) that need the authenticated session from cookies.

**Inline `createClient` in public pages — unauthenticated:**
```ts
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'  // raw JS client, NOT @supabase/ssr
const supabasePublic = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey)
```
Used in: every page under `app/public/` that doesn't need auth. Named `supabasePublic` by convention. Also used in some layout files for metadata generation. This is the correct approach for a public booking page.

---

## Key Patterns Summary for a Booking System

Based on what exists:

1. **Page structure:** Create `app/public/book/[slug]/page.tsx` as a `'use client'` component. Use a layout file at `app/public/book/[slug]/layout.tsx` for metadata (following the forms layout pattern).

2. **Supabase client:** Instantiate `createClient(url, anonKey)` inline as `supabasePublic` — same as forms and portal pages. No auth required for viewing, but writes go through RLS.

3. **User lookup by slug:** The `users` table has no `slug` column — you would need to look up by a different identifier (e.g., `telegram_id`, `x_id`, or a new `calendly_url` column), or add a `slug` column to `users`.

4. **Saving a booking:** Either (a) write directly via `supabasePublic` to a `bookings` table with an RLS policy allowing anon inserts, or (b) POST to an internal API route (`/api/bookings/submit`) for server-side validation (the pattern used by `leads/submit`).

5. **Linking to an opportunity:** Call `SalesPipelineService.update(id, { calendly_booked_date, next_meeting_at, next_meeting_type })` — these fields already exist on the `crm_opportunities` table.

     1→import { Metadata } from 'next';
     2→import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
     3→
     4→const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!;
     5→const supabaseAnonKey = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!;
     6→
     7→type Props = {
     8→  params: { id: string };
     9→  children: React.ReactNode;
    10→};
    11→
    12→export async function generateMetadata({ params }: Props): Promise<Metadata> {
    13→  const supabase = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey);
    14→
    15→  // Fetch form data
    16→  const { data: form, error } = await supabase
    17→    .from('forms')
    18→    .select('id, name')
    19→    .eq('id', params.id)
    20→    .single();
    21→
    22→  const title = form?.name || 'Holo Hive Portal';
    23→  const description = 'Form';
    24→
    25→  // Construct the base URL for the logo
    26→  const baseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL
    27→    ? (process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL.startsWith('http')
    28→        ? process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL
    29→        : `https://${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL}`)
    30→    : (process.env.VERCEL_URL ? `https://${process.env.VERCEL_URL}` : 'http://localhost:3000');
    31→  const logoUrl = `${baseUrl}/images/logo.png`;
    32→
    33→  return {
    34→    title,
    35→    description,
    36→    metadataBase: new URL(baseUrl),
    37→    robots: {
    38→      index: false,
    39→      follow: false,
    40→      googleBot: {
    41→        index: false,
    42→        follow: false,
    43→      },
    44→    },
    45→    openGraph: {
    46→      title,
    47→      description,
    48→      images: [
    49→        {
    50→          url: logoUrl,
    51→          width: 500,
    52→          height: 500,
    53→          alt: 'Holo Hive Logo',
    54→        },
    55→      ],
    56→      type: 'website',
    57→    },
    58→    twitter: {
    59→      card: 'summary',
    60→      title,
    61→      description,
    62→      images: [logoUrl],
    63→    },
    64→    other: {
    65→      'og:image': logoUrl,
    66→      'og:image:width': '500',
    67→      'og:image:height': '500',
    68→      'og:image:type': 'image/png',
    69→    },
    70→  };
    71→}
    72→
    73→export default function PublicFormLayout({ children }: Props) {
    74→  return <>{children}</>;
    75→}
    76→
