```text
   154→   * Activate user (admin only)
   155→   */
   156→  static async activateUser(userId: string): Promise<boolean> {
   157→    return this.updateUserProfile(userId, { is_active: true })
   158→  }
   159→
   160→  /**
   161→   * Delete user record (admin only, for rejecting pending sign-ups)
   162→   */
   163→  static async deleteUser(userId: string): Promise<boolean> {
   164→    try {
   165→      const { error } = await supabase
   166→        .from('users')
   167→        .delete()
   168→        .eq('id', userId)
   169→
   170→      if (error) {
   171→        console.error('Error deleting user:', error)
   172→        return false
   173→      }
   174→
   175→      return true
   176→    } catch (error) {
   177→      console.error('Error deleting user:', error)
   178→      return false
   179→    }
   180→  }
   181→
   182→  /**
   183→   * Check if current user is admin
   184→   */
   185→  static async isCurrentUserAdmin(): Promise<boolean> {
   186→    try {
   187→      const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser()
   188→      if (!user) return false
   189→
   190→      const profile = await this.getUserProfile(user.id)
   191→      return (profile?.role === 'admin' || profile?.role === 'super_admin') && profile?.is_active === true
   192→    } catch (error) {
   193→      console.error('Error checking admin status:', error)
   194→      return false
   195→    }
   196→  }
   197→
   198→  /**
   199→   * Get current user's profile
   200→   */
   201→  static async getCurrentUserProfile(): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
   202→    try {
   203→      const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser()
   204→      if (!user) return null
   205→
   206→      return await this.getUserProfile(user.id)
   207→    } catch (error) {
   208→      console.error('Error getting current user profile:', error)
   209→      return null
   210→    }
   211→  }
   212→}
   213→
   214→export type { UserProfile, UserInsert, UserUpdate }
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→import { supabase } from './supabase';
     2→
     3→// ============================================
     4→// TypeScript Types for CRM Entities
     5→// ============================================
     6→
     7→// Opportunity Stages
     8→export type OpportunityStage =
     9→  | 'new' | 'contacted' | 'qualified' | 'unqualified' | 'nurture' | 'dead'  // Lead stages
    10→  | 'deal_qualified' | 'proposal' | 'negotiation' | 'contract' | 'closed_won' | 'closed_lost'  // Deal stages
    11→  | 'account_active' | 'account_at_risk' | 'account_churned'  // Account stages
    12→  | 'cold_dm' | 'warm' | 'tg_intro' | 'booked' | 'discovery_done' | 'proposal_sent' | 'proposal_call' | 'v2_contract' | 'v2_closed_won' | 'orbit' | 'v2_closed_lost';  // Sales Pipeline v2 stages
    13→
    14→export type AccountType = 'general' | 'channel' | 'campaign' | 'lite' | 'ad_hoc';
    15→export type OpportunitySource = 'referral' | 'inbound' | 'event' | 'cold_outreach';
    16→
    17→export type AffiliateStatus = 'new' | 'active' | 'inactive';
    18→export type PartnerStatus = 'active' | 'inactive';
    19→export type PartnerCategory = 'service_provider' | 'investor_vc' | 'project' | 'individual';
    20→
    21→export type LinkedType = 'opportunity' | 'partner' | 'affiliate';
    22→
    23→// ============================================
    24→// Interfaces
    25→// ============================================
    26→
    27→export interface CRMAffiliate {
    28→  id: string;
    29→  name: string;
    30→  affiliation: string | null;
    31→  category: string | null;
    32→  status: AffiliateStatus;
    33→  commission_model: string | null;
    34→  commission_rate: number | null;
    35→  terms_of_interest: string | null;
    36→  last_contacted_at: string | null;
    37→  owner_id: string | null;
    38→  poc_name: string | null;
    39→  poc_email: string | null;
    40→  poc_telegram: string | null;
    41→  notes: string | null;
    42→  created_at: string;
    43→  updated_at: string;
    44→}
    45→
    46→export interface CRMOpportunity {
    47→  id: string;
    48→  name: string;
    49→  stage: OpportunityStage;
    50→  account_type: AccountType | null;
    51→  deal_value: number | null;
    52→  currency: string;
    53→  last_contacted_at: string | null;
    54→  last_message_at: string | null;       // When lead/others last messaged in TG group
    55→  last_reply_at: string | null;          // When our bot last messaged in TG group
    56→  last_team_message_at: string | null;   // When a team member last messaged in TG group
    57→  owner_id: string | null;
    58→  source: OpportunitySource | null;
    59→  referrer: string | null;
    60→  gc: string | null;               // Telegram group chat ID
    61→  affiliate_id: string | null;
    62→  client_id: string | null;        // Links account-stage opportunities to clients
    63→  scope: string | null;            // Scope for accounts: fundraising, advisory, kol_activation, gtm, bd_partnerships, apac
    64→  notes: string | null;
    65→  position: number;
    66→  created_at: string;
    67→  updated_at: string;
    68→  qualified_at: string | null;
    69→  closed_at: string | null;
    70→  // Joined data
    71→  affiliate?: CRMAffiliate | null;
    72→  contacts?: CRMContact[];
    73→  client?: { id: string; name: string } | null;
    74→}
    75→
    76→export interface CRMPartner {
    77→  id: string;
    78→  name: string;
    79→  category: PartnerCategory | null;
    80→  focus: string | null;
    81→  status: PartnerStatus;
    82→  last_contacted_at: string | null;
    83→  owner_id: string | null;
    84→  poc_name: string | null;
    85→  poc_email: string | null;
    86→  poc_telegram: string | null;
    87→  is_affiliate: boolean;
    88→  affiliate_id: string | null;
    89→  notes: string | null;
    90→  created_at: string;
    91→  updated_at: string;
    92→  // Joined data
    93→  affiliate?: CRMAffiliate | null;
    94→  contacts?: CRMContact[];
    95→}
    96→
    97→export interface CRMContact {
    98→  id: string;
    99→  name: string;
   100→  email: string | null;
```

```text
     1→'use client';

    11→import { Checkbox } from '@/components/ui/checkbox';

    16→
    17→// Create a standalone Supabase client for public access
    18→const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!;
    19→const supabaseAnonKey = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!;
    20→const supabase = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey);
    21→
    22→const ACCOUNT_TYPE_OPTIONS = [
    23→  { value: 'project', label: 'Project/Protocol' },
    24→  { value: 'exchange', label: 'Exchange' },
    25→  { value: 'fund', label: 'Fund/VC' },
    26→  { value: 'agency', label: 'Agency' },
    27→  { value: 'individual', label: 'Individual' },
    28→  { value: 'other', label: 'Other' }
    29→];
    30→
    31→const SOURCE_OPTIONS = [
    32→  { value: 'referral', label: 'Referral' },
    33→  { value: 'website', label: 'Website' },
    34→  { value: 'twitter', label: 'Twitter/X' },
    35→  { value: 'telegram', label: 'Telegram' },
    36→  { value: 'linkedin', label: 'LinkedIn' },
    37→  { value: 'conference', label: 'Conference/Event' },
    38→  { value: 'cold_outreach', label: 'Cold Outreach' },
    39→  { value: 'other', label: 'Other' }
    40→];
    41→
    42→const SCOPE_OPTIONS = [
    43→  { value: 'kol_activation', label: 'KOL Activation' },
    44→  { value: 'gtm', label: 'Go-to-Market (GTM)' },
    45→  { value: 'advisory', label: 'Advisory' },
    46→  { value: 'bd_partnerships', label: 'BD/Partnerships' },
    47→  { value: 'fundraising', label: 'Fundraising' },
    48→  { value: 'apac', label: 'APAC Expansion' }
    49→];
    50→
    51→const CURRENCY_OPTIONS = [
    52→  { value: 'USD', label: 'USD' },
    53→  { value: 'USDT', label: 'USDT' },
    54→  { value: 'USDC', label: 'USDC' },
    55→  { value: 'ETH', label: 'ETH' },
    56→  { value: 'BTC', label: 'BTC' }
    57→];
    58→
    59→export default function LeadSubmitPage() {
    60→  const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting] = useState(false);
    61→  const [isSubmitted, setIsSubmitted] = useState(false);
    62→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
    63→  const [scopePopoverOpen, setScopePopoverOpen] = useState(false);
    64→  const [referrerPopoverOpen, setReferrerPopoverOpen] = useState(false);
    65→  const [existingReferrers, setExistingReferrers] = useState<string[]>([]);
    66→
    67→  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({
    68→    name: '',
    69→    account_type: '',
    70→    scope: [] as string[],
    71→    deal_value: '',
    72→    currency: 'USD',
    73→    source: '',
    74→    referrer: '',
    75→    gc: '',
    76→    notes: '',
    77→    contact_name: '',
    78→    contact_email: '',
    79→    contact_telegram: ''
    80→  });
    81→
    82→  // Fetch existing referrers for suggestions
    83→  useEffect(() => {
    84→    const fetchReferrers = async () => {
    85→      const { data } = await supabase
    86→        .from('crm_opportunities')
    87→        .select('referrer')
    88→        .not('referrer', 'is', null);
    89→
    90→      if (data) {
    91→        const referrers = new Set<string>();
    92→        data.forEach((opp: { referrer: string | null }) => {
    93→          if (opp.referrer && opp.referrer.trim()) {
    94→            referrers.add(opp.referrer.trim());
    95→          }
    96→        });
    97→        setExistingReferrers(Array.from(referrers).sort());
    98→      }
    99→    };
   100→    fetchReferrers();
   101→  }, []);
   102→
   103→  const referrerSuggestions = useMemo(() => {
   104→    return existingReferrers;
   105→  }, [existingReferrers]);
   106→
   107→  const toggleScope = (scope: string) => {
   108→    setFormData(prev => ({
   109→      ...prev,
   110→      scope: prev.scope.includes(scope)
   111→        ? prev.scope.filter(s => s !== scope)
   112→        : [...prev.scope, scope]
   113→    }));
   114→  };
   115→
   116→  const getScopeLabel = (value: string) => {
   117→    return SCOPE_OPTIONS.find(s => s.value === value)?.label || value;
   118→  };
   119→
   120→  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
   121→    e.preventDefault();
   122→    setError(null);
   123→
   124→    if (!formData.name.trim()) {
   125→      setError('Lead/Opportunity name is required');
   126→      return;
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    if (!formData.contact_name.trim()) {
   130→      setError('Contact name is required');
   131→      return;
   132→    }
   133→
   134→    if (!formData.contact_email.trim() && !formData.contact_telegram.trim()) {
   135→      setError('At least one contact method (email or Telegram) is required');
   136→      return;
   137→    }
   138→
   139→    if (formData.contact_email && !/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(formData.contact_email)) {
   140→      setError('Please enter a valid email address');
   141→      return;
   142→    }
   143→
   144→    if (formData.deal_value && isNaN(parseFloat(formData.deal_value))) {
   145→      setError('Deal value must be a valid number');
   146→      return;
   147→    }
   148→
   149→    setIsSubmitting(true);
   150→
   151→    try {
   152→      const response = await fetch('/api/leads/submit', {
   153→        method: 'POST',
   154→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   155→        body: JSON.stringify(formData)
   156→      });
   157→
   158→      const result = await response.json();
   159→
   160→      if (!response.ok) {
   161→        throw new Error(result.error || 'Failed to submit lead');
   162→      }
   163→
   164→      setIsSubmitted(true);
   165→    } catch (err: any) {
   166→      setError(err.message || 'An error occurred while submitting');
   167→    } finally {
   168→      setIsSubmitting(false);
   169→    }
   170→  };
   171→
   172→  if (isSubmitted) {
   173→    return (
   174→      <div className="min-h-screen bg-gradient-to-br from-gray-50 to-gray-100 flex items-center justify-center p-4">
   175→        <div className="w-full max-w-md bg-white rounded-2xl shadow-xl border border-gray-100 p-8">
   176→          <div className="text-center">
   177→            <div className="mx-auto w-16 h-16 bg-green-100 rounded-full flex items-center justify-center mb-6">
   178→              <Check className="h-8 w-8 text-green-600" />
   179→            </div>
   180→            <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mb-2">Lead Submitted!</h2>
   181→            <p className="text-gray-500 mb-8">Thank you for your submission. Our team will review and follow up shortly.</p>
   182→            <Button
   183→              onClick={() => {
   184→                setIsSubmitted(false);
   185→                setFormData({
   186→                  name: '',
   187→                  account_type: '',
   188→                  scope: [],
   189→                  deal_value: '',
   190→                  currency: 'USD',
   191→                  source: '',
   192→                  referrer: '',
   193→                  gc: '',
   194→                  notes: '',
   195→                  contact_name: '',
   196→                  contact_email: '',
   197→                  contact_telegram: ''
   198→                });
   199→              }}
   200→              variant="outline"
   201→              className="px-6"
   202→            >
   203→              Submit Another Lead
   204→            </Button>
   205→          </div>
   206→        </div>
   207→      </div>
   208→    );
   209→  }
   210→
   211→  return (
   212→    <div className="min-h-screen bg-gradient-to-br from-gray-50 to-gray-100 flex items-center justify-center p-4">
   213→      <div className="w-full max-w-lg">
   214→        {/* Header with logo */}
   215→        <div className="text-center mb-6">
   216→          <div className="inline-flex items-center justify-center mb-4">
   217→            <Image
   218→              src="/images/logo.png"
   219→              alt="Logo"
   220→              width={48}
   221→              height={48}
   222→              className="rounded-lg"
   223→            />
   224→          </div>
   225→          <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Submit a Lead</h1>
   226→          <p className="text-gray-500 mt-1">Share a potential opportunity with us</p>
   227→        </div>
   228→
   229→        {/* Form Card */}
   230→        <div className="bg-white rounded-2xl shadow-xl border border-gray-100 p-6 sm:p-8">
   231→          <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="space-y-5">
   232→            {error && (
   233→              <div className="bg-red-50 border border-red-200 text-red-700 px-4 py-3 rounded-lg text-sm">
   234→                {error}
   235→              </div>
   236→            )}
   237→
   238→            <div className="space-y-2">
   239→              <Label htmlFor="name">Lead/Opportunity Name <span className="text-red-500">*</span></Label>
   240→              <Input
   241→                id="name"
   242→                placeholder="Enter company or project name"
   243→                value={formData.name}
   244→                onChange={e => setFormData(prev => ({ ...prev, name: e.target.value }))}
   245→                disabled={isSubmitting}
   246→                className="auth-input"
   247→              />
   248→            </div>
   249→
   250→            <div className="space-y-2">
   251→              <Label>Account Type</Label>
   252→              <Select
   253→                value={formData.account_type}
   254→                onValueChange={(value) => setFormData(prev => ({ ...prev, account_type: value }))}
   255→                disabled={isSubmitting}
   256→              >
   257→                <SelectTrigger className="auth-input">
   258→                  <SelectValue placeholder="Select account type..." />
   259→                </SelectTrigger>
   260→                <SelectContent>
   261→                  {ACCOUNT_TYPE_OPTIONS.map(opt => (
   262→                    <SelectItem key={opt.value} value={opt.value}>{opt.label}</SelectItem>
   263→                  ))}
   264→                </SelectContent>
   265→              </Select>
   266→            </div>
   267→
   268→            <div className="space-y-2">
   269→              <Label>Scope of Interest</Label>
   270→              <Popover open={scopePopoverOpen} onOpenChange={setScopePopoverOpen}>
   271→                <PopoverTrigger asChild>
   272→                  <Button
   273→                    variant="outline"
   274→                    role="combobox"
   275→                    className="w-full justify-between font-normal auth-input"
   276→                    disabled={isSubmitting}
   277→                  >
   278→                    {formData.scope.length > 0
   279→                      ? `${formData.scope.length} selected`
   280→                      : 'Select scope...'}
   281→                    <ChevronsUpDown className="ml-2 h-4 w-4 shrink-0 opacity-50" />
   282→                  </Button>
   283→                </PopoverTrigger>
   284→                <PopoverContent className="w-[400px] p-0">
   285→                  <Command>
```

```text
     1→'use client';
     2→
     3→import { useState, useEffect, useMemo } from 'react';
     4→import { useRouter } from 'next/navigation';
     5→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
     6→import { Label } from '@/components/ui/label';
     7→import { Textarea } from '@/components/ui/textarea';
     8→import { Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue } from '@/components/ui/select';
     9→import { Check, Loader2 } from 'lucide-react';
    10→import Image from 'next/image';
    11→import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext';
    12→import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase';
    13→
    14→export default function StandupSubmitPage() {
    15→  const { user, userProfile, loading: authLoading } = useAuth();
    16→  const router = useRouter();
    17→
    18→  const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting] = useState(false);
    19→  const [isSubmitted, setIsSubmitted] = useState(false);
    20→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
    21→  const [hasSubmittedToday, setHasSubmittedToday] = useState(false);
    22→  const [checkingToday, setCheckingToday] = useState(true);
    23→
    24→  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({
    25→    completed_yesterday: '',
    26→    priorities: '',
    27→    output_goal: '',
    28→    blockers: '',
    29→  });
    30→
    31→  const todayStr = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];
    32→
    33→  // Redirect to login if not authenticated
    34→  useEffect(() => {
    35→    if (!authLoading && !user) {
    36→      router.push('/auth?redirectTo=/public/standup/submit');
    37→    }
    38→  }, [authLoading, user, router]);
    39→
    40→  // Check if already submitted today
    41→  useEffect(() => {
    42→    if (!user) return;
    43→    const checkToday = async () => {
    44→      const { data } = await supabase
    45→        .from('daily_standups')
    46→        .select('id')
    47→        .eq('user_id', user.id)
    48→        .eq('submission_date', todayStr)
    49→        .limit(1);
    50→      setHasSubmittedToday((data && data.length > 0) || false);
    51→      setCheckingToday(false);
    52→    };
    53→    checkToday();
    54→  }, [user, todayStr]);
    55→
    56→  const userName = useMemo(() => {
    57→    if (userProfile?.name) return userProfile.name;
    58→    if (userProfile?.email) return userProfile.email;
    59→    if (user?.email) return user.email;
    60→    return 'Unknown';
```

 `./lib/slugUtils.ts`

```text
     1→import { nanoid } from 'nanoid';
     2→
     3→/**
     4→ * Generate a short random slug using nanoid
     5→ * Default length is 8 characters (62^8 = 218 trillion possibilities)
     6→ */
     7→export function generateSlug(length: number = 8): string {
     8→  return nanoid(length);
     9→}
    10→
    11→/**
    12→ * Convert a name to a URL-friendly slug
    13→ * E.g., "My Cool Form!" -> "my-cool-form"
    14→ */
    15→export function nameToSlug(name: string): string {
    16→  return name
    17→    .toLowerCase()
    18→    .trim()
    19→    .replace(/[^\w\s-]/g, '') // Remove special characters
    20→    .replace(/\s+/g, '-') // Replace spaces with hyphens
    21→    .replace(/-+/g, '-') // Replace multiple hyphens with single
    22→    .substring(0, 50); // Limit length
    23→}
    24→
    25→/**
    26→ * Generate a slug from name with a random suffix to ensure uniqueness
    27→ * E.g., "My Form" -> "my-form-x7k2m"
    28→ */
    29→export function generateUniqueSlug(name?: string, suffixLength: number = 5): string {
    30→  if (name) {
    31→    const baseSlug = nameToSlug(name);
    32→    const suffix = nanoid(suffixLength);
    33→    return `${baseSlug}-${suffix}`;
    34→  }
    35→  return generateSlug(8);
    36→}
    37→
    38→/**
    39→ * Validate a slug format (alphanumeric with hyphens)
    40→ */
    41→export function isValidSlug(slug: string): boolean {
    42→  return /^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$/.test(slug);
    43→}
    44→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-06 03:47:36Z)**_

I now have a comprehensive picture of all the patterns. Let me compile the complete findings.

---

Here is a thorough breakdown of all five areas you asked about.

---

## 1. How Public Pages Work

**File tree under `/app/public/`:**
```
app/public/
  layout.tsx                    # Root public layout (no auth, robots noindex)
  page.tsx                      # Index page
  forms/[id]/
    layout.tsx                  # Fetches form name for <head> metadata
    page.tsx                    # Full form renderer (client component)
  portal/[id]/
    layout.tsx                  # Minimal layout
    page.tsx                    # Client portal viewer (client component)
  campaigns/[id]/               # Public campaign view
  reports/[id]/                 # Public report view
  lists/[id]/                   # Public list view
  leads/submit/page.tsx         # Lead submission form (no auth required)
  links/submit/page.tsx         # Link submission form (no auth required)
  partners/submit/page.tsx      # Partner submission form (no auth required)
  standup/submit/page.tsx       # Stand-up form (REQUIRES auth - redirects to /auth)
```

**Auth handling — the two patterns:**

**Pattern A: Fully public (no auth needed).** Used by `forms/[id]`, `leads/submit`, `links/submit`, `partners/submit`, `portal/[id]`:
- A raw `createClient(url, anonKey)` is created inline at the top of the client component — this is the "supabasePublic" client.
- No session check is done. The anon key's RLS policies gate what data can be read/written.
- Named `supabasePublic` in form and portal pages.

```ts
// Pattern A — from app/public/forms/[id]/page.tsx and portal/[id]/page.tsx
const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!;
const supabaseAnonKey = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!;
const supabasePublic = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey);
// import is: import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
```

The layout files use the same bare `createClient` for SSR metadata generation (e.g., fetching the form name in `generateMetadata`).

**Pattern B: Auth-gated public route.** Used by `standup/submit`:
- Uses the shared `supabase` browser client from `@/lib/supabase` (cookie-based, session-aware).
- Imports `useAuth` from `@/contexts/AuthContext`.
- Redirects unauthenticated users: `router.push('/auth?redirectTo=/public/standup/submit')`.

**Form submission approaches:**
- `forms/[id]` calls `FormService.submitResponse(...)` passing the `supabasePublic` client directly.
- `leads/submit` POSTs to `/api/leads/submit` (an internal Next.js API route) — the API route handles the actual Supabase insert with server-side privileges.
- `links/submit` and `partners/submit` use the inline `createClient` directly.

**Root public layout** (`/app/public/layout.tsx`): Minimal — just `min-h-screen bg-gray-50`, no nav, no auth context. Sets `robots: { index: false }` to block search engines.

---

## 2. Users Table Structure

**From `/lib/database.types.ts` (line 914):**

```ts
users: {
  Row: {
    id: string                    // UUID (matches Supabase auth.users.id)
    email: string
    name: string
    role: string                  // 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'guest'
    is_active: boolean
    profile_photo_url: string | null
    telegram_id: string | null
    x_id: string | null
    created_at: string | null
    updated_at: string | null
  }
  // Insert/Update types mirror the Row with optionals
}
```

**Notable: there is NO `slug` column on `users`.** The `slug` concept exists on `clients` (from the portal page's `Client` type: `slug: string | null`), not users.

**How users are fetched and listed:**

In `UserService` (`/lib/userService.ts`):
- `getAllUsers()` — fetches all rows from `users`, ordered by `created_at desc`
- `getUserProfile(userId)` — fetches one user by UUID
- `getCurrentUserProfile()` — calls `supabase.auth.getUser()` then `getUserProfile`
- Role hierarchy: `guest < member < admin < super_admin`
- New OAuth sign-ups are created with `is_active: false`, require admin approval

In the sales pipeline, users are used for owner dropdowns:
```ts
// From sales-pipeline/page.tsx
const [users, setUsers] = useState<{ id: string; name: string | null; email: string }[]>([]);
// ...
UserService.getAllUsers()  // returns full UserProfile[], used for owner assignment UI
```

The `TeamMember` interface in `app/team/page.tsx` has: `id, name, email, role, is_active, telegram_id, x_id, profile_photo_url`.

---

## 3. Sales Pipeline Opportunity Meeting Fields

**From `/lib/salesPipelineService.ts`:**

The `SalesPipelineOpportunity` interface **extends** `CRMOpportunity` with these meeting/calendly fields:

```ts
export interface SalesPipelineOpportunity extends CRMOpportunity {
  // Calendly tracking fields
  calendly_sent_via: CalendlySentVia;        // 'dm' | 'tg' | 'not_yet'
  calendly_sent_date: string | null;          // ISO datetime
  calendly_booked_date: string | null;        // ISO datetime — when they confirmed a slot

  // Next meeting scheduling
  next_meeting_at: string | null;             // ISO datetime — when next meeting is
  next_meeting_type: string | null;           // free-form string (e.g., 'discovery', 'proposal')

  // Other pipeline-specific timestamps
  bucket_changed_at: string | null;
  discovery_call_at: string | null;           // Auto-set when stage -> discovery_done
  proposal_sent_at: string | null;            // Auto-set when stage -> proposal_sent

  // Other fields
  bucket: Bucket | null;                      // 'A' | 'B' | 'C'
  temperature_score: number;
  dm_account: DmAccount;                      // 'closer' | 'sdr' | 'other'
  bump_number: number;
  last_bump_date: string | null;
  warm_sub_state: WarmSubState | null;        // 'interested' | 'silent'
  tg_handle: string | null;
  gc_opened: string;
  orbit_reason: OrbitReason | null;
  closed_lost_reason: string | null;
  dedup_key: string | null;
  poc_platform: PocPlatform | null;
  poc_handle: string | null;
}
```

**The `update()` method signature** includes all meeting fields:
```ts
static async update(id, updates: Partial<...> & {
  next_meeting_at?: string | null;
  next_meeting_type?: string | null;
  calendly_sent_via?: CalendlySentVia;
  calendly_sent_date?: string | null;
  calendly_booked_date?: string | null;
  // ...
})
```

**BAMFAM enforcement:** The service has `getBAMFAMViolations()` — it queries `discovery_done`, `proposal_call`, `v2_contract` stage opportunities where `next_meeting_at IS NULL` or `next_meeting_at < now()`. The metrics also count this number for the dashboard.

**`CalendlySentVia` type:** `'dm' | 'tg' | 'not_yet'` — tracks whether the Calendly link was sent via DM, Telegram, or hasn't been sent.

The underlying DB table is `crm_opportunities` (shared with the general CRM, filtered to sales pipeline v2 stages).

---

## 4. Settings Pages / Admin Config Patterns

**Only one settings page found:** `/app/settings/page.tsx`

Pattern used:
```ts
// Wrapped in ProtectedRoute — only accessible to authenticated users
export default function SettingsPage() {
  return (
    <ProtectedRoute>
      <SettingsContent />
    </ProtectedRoute>
  );
}

// Inner component uses the session-aware browser client
function SettingsContent() {
  const { user, userProfile, refreshUserProfile } = useAuth();
  const { toast } = useToast();

  // Profile fields editable: name, email, telegram_id, x_id, profile_photo_url
  // Admin/super_admin also see a Telegram webhook management section
}
```

The settings page uses `supabase` from `@/lib/supabase` (the cookie-based browser client), not `supabasePublic`. It calls Supabase auth methods (`supabase.auth.getSession()`) and passes the access token to internal API routes for privileged operations like webhook management.

---

## 5. Supabase Client Setup in `/lib/`

Three client variants exist:

**`/lib/supabase.ts` — Browser/client-side (auth-aware):**
```ts
import { createBrowserClient } from '@supabase/ssr'
import { Database } from './database.types'

// Uses @supabase/ssr — cookie-based session, works across client/server boundary
export const supabase = createBrowserClient<Database>(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey)
```
Used in: all `'use client'` components that need the user's session (settings, standup, CRM pages, team page).

**`/lib/supabase-server.ts` — Server-side (API routes, Server Components):**
```ts
import { createServerClient as createSupabaseServerClient } from '@supabase/ssr';
import { cookies } from 'next/headers';

export async function createServerClient() {
  const cookieStore = await cookies();
  return createSupabaseServerClient<Database>(url, anonKey, {
    cookies: {
      getAll() { return cookieStore.getAll(); },
      setAll(cookiesToSet) { /* sets cookies, ignores errors in Server Components */ }
    }
  });
}
```
Used in: API routes (`/api/...`) that need the authenticated session from cookies.

**Inline `createClient` in public pages — unauthenticated:**
```ts
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'  // raw JS client, NOT @supabase/ssr
const supabasePublic = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey)
```
Used in: every page under `app/public/` that doesn't need auth. Named `supabasePublic` by convention. Also used in some layout files for metadata generation. This is the correct approach for a public booking page.

---

## Key Patterns Summary for a Booking System

Based on what exists:

1. **Page structure:** Create `app/public/book/[slug]/page.tsx` as a `'use client'` component. Use a layout file at `app/public/book/[slug]/layout.tsx` for metadata (following the forms layout pattern).

2. **Supabase client:** Instantiate `createClient(url, anonKey)` inline as `supabasePublic` — same as forms and portal pages. No auth required for viewing, but writes go through RLS.

3. **User lookup by slug:** The `users` table has no `slug` column — you would need to look up by a different identifier (e.g., `telegram_id`, `x_id`, or a new `calendly_url` column), or add a `slug` column to `users`.

4. **Saving a booking:** Either (a) write directly via `supabasePublic` to a `bookings` table with an RLS policy allowing anon inserts, or (b) POST to an internal API route (`/api/bookings/submit`) for server-side validation (the pattern used by `leads/submit`).

5. **Linking to an opportunity:** Call `SalesPipelineService.update(id, { calendly_booked_date, next_meeting_at, next_meeting_type })` — these fields already exist on the `crm_opportunities` table.

- Users table needs a `booking_slug` column
- Public page at `/app/public/book/[slug]/page.tsx`
- Admin config somewhere (maybe settings page or a new booking management page)
- Auto-link to opportunities via unique booking URL with opportunity ID
- Fixed time slots, customizable per user

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-06 03:48:21Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
# Custom Booking System

## Context
The team currently sends Calendly links to prospects/clients for scheduling meetings. This creates a disconnect — bookings happen outside the system, so `next_meeting_at` and other opportunity fields must be manually updated. Building a simple in-house booking page at `app.holohive.io/public/book/[slug]` will auto-link bookings to opportunities and update pipeline fields automatically. Each team member gets their own booking page with customizable fixed time slots.

## Database Migration

### New tables

**`booking_pages`** — one per team member
| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| id | uuid PK | |
| user_id | uuid FK → users.id | owner |
| slug | text UNIQUE NOT NULL | URL identifier (e.g. `yano`, `jdot`, `andy`) |
| title | text | Display name (e.g. "Book a call with Yano") |
| description | text | Optional subtitle |
| slot_duration_minutes | int | Default 30 |
| available_slots | jsonb | Array of `{ day: 0-6, start: "HH:MM", end: "HH:MM" }` (UTC) |
| is_active | boolean DEFAULT true | Can disable page |
| created_at / updated_at | timestamptz | |

**`bookings`** — each scheduled meeting
| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| id | uuid PK | |
| booking_page_id | uuid FK → booking_pages.id | |
| opportunity_id | uuid FK → crm_opportunities.id NULL | Auto-link when URL has `?opp=<id>` |
| booker_name | text | Guest's name |
| booker_email | text | Guest's email |
| meeting_date | date | |
| start_time | time | UTC |
| end_time | time | UTC |
| status | text | `confirmed`, `cancelled` |
| notes | text | Optional message from booker |
| created_at | timestamptz | |

### RLS Policies
- `booking_pages`: anon SELECT (for public page), authenticated INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE (owner or admin)
- `bookings`: anon INSERT + SELECT (public can book and check availability), authenticated full access

### Seed data
- Create a booking page for each active user with a slug derived from their name (lowercase first name or custom)
- Default available_slots: Mon-Fri, 09:00-17:00 UTC, 30-min slots

## Files to Create/Modify

### 1. `lib/bookingService.ts` (NEW)
- `getBookingPageBySlug(slug)` — fetch page + owner info (public, uses supabasePublic)
- `getAvailableSlots(bookingPageId, date)` — compute open slots from `available_slots` minus existing `bookings` for that date
- `createBooking(data)` — insert booking, optionally update opportunity (`next_meeting_at`, `calendly_booked_date`, stage → `booked` if currently in outreach stages)
- `getBookingsByPage(bookingPageId, dateRange)` — for admin view
- `updateBookingPage(id, updates)` — update availability config
- `cancelBooking(id)` — set status to cancelled

### 2. `app/public/book/[slug]/page.tsx` (NEW)
Public booking page (no auth). Pattern: inline `createClient` as `supabasePublic`.

**Flow:**
1. Fetch booking page by slug → show owner name, title, description
2. Date picker (calendar) — show next 14 days
3. On date select → show available time slots as buttons
4. On slot select → form: name, email, optional notes
5. Submit → create booking → success screen with meeting details
6. URL supports `?opp=<opportunity_id>` to auto-link

**UI:** Clean, minimal. Teal accent (`#3e8692`). HoloHive logo. Mobile-friendly.

### 3. `app/public/book/[slug]/layout.tsx` (NEW)
SSR metadata — fetch booking page title for `<head>`.

### 4. `app/settings/page.tsx` (MODIFY)
Add "Booking Page" section for each user to manage their own booking page:
- Toggle active/inactive
- Edit title and description
- View/copy public booking URL
- Configure available time slots (day-of-week checkboxes + start/end time pickers)
- Slot duration selector (15/30/45/60 min)

### 5. `app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx` (MODIFY)
- In the opportunity detail/edit modal, add a "Copy Booking Link" button that generates `app.holohive.io/public/book/[owner-slug]?opp=[opportunity-id]`
- When a booking comes in with `opp` param, auto-update:
  - `next_meeting_at` → booking datetime
  - `calendly_booked_date` → now
  - `next_meeting_type` → 'discovery' (default)

### 6. Telegram notification (in migration)
- Add trigger `notify_new_booking()` on `bookings` INSERT → sends Telegram message with booker name, meeting time, and opportunity name if linked

## Key Decisions
- **No Google Meet integration for now** — just scheduling. Meet links can be added manually or in a future iteration.
- **Fixed time slots** from `available_slots` JSON — not calendar-synced. Simple and customizable.
- **UTC throughout** — all times stored and displayed in UTC as requested.
- **Slug on `booking_pages`** not on `users` — keeps user table clean, allows custom booking slugs.
- **`?opp=` query param** links booking to opportunity — simple, no complex routing needed.

## Verification
1. Visit `/public/book/yano` → see Yano's booking page with available slots
2. Pick a date → see available time slots
3. Book a slot → success screen, booking appears in DB
4. Same slot no longer available for that date
5. If URL had `?opp=<id>`, opportunity's `next_meeting_at` is updated
6. Telegram notification fires
7. Settings page → user can toggle availability, change slot times
8. Sales pipeline → "Copy Booking Link" button generates correct URL
```
